sources: Georgia Office of the Insurance Commissioner diminished value guidance (17c methodology)
Georgia unfair claims practices and total loss handling (industry practice)

diminished_value_17c.regulation_reference: Georgia OIC diminished value / 17c worksheet methodology (insurer first-party repairable vehicle claims)
diminished_value_17c.description: Georgia commonly applies a three-part estimate: base value loss (10% cap on pre-accident FMV) multiplied by a damage-severity factor (from repair cost as a percentage of FMV) and a mileage factor (NADA-style brackets). This file documents the brackets implemented in code; adjust when OIC publishes updates.

provisions.title: Base value loss cap
provisions.requirement: Base value loss = 10% × pre-accident fair market value (ACV) before applying damage and mileage multipliers.

provisions.title: Damage multiplier from repair cost ÷ FMV
provisions.requirement: Repair cost as a fraction of FMV maps to a damage multiplier: under 20% → 0; 20% to under 40% → 0.10; 40% to under 60% → 0.20; 60% to under 80% → 0.40; 80% to under 95% → 0.60; 95%+ → 1.00.

provisions.title: Mileage multiplier (NADA-style brackets)
provisions.requirement: Mileage odometer reading maps to a multiplier applied after base and damage factors.

provisions.title: Qualitative damage tiers (when repair cost unknown)
provisions.requirement: When itemized repair cost is not available, use damage_severity_tier: cosmetic/light → 0.10; moderate → 0.40; major → 0.60; structural → 0.80; severe → 1.00. Unknown labels default to moderate (0.40).

provisions.title: Combined estimate
provisions.requirement: Estimated diminished value = base_value_loss × damage_multiplier × mileage_multiplier (rounded to cents).
